understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each KA59 level by moving the selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked with a '+' was occupied by a selectable piece at the same time, progressing through all levels until the game reports WIN.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn controlled the currently selected piece (shown as 'P' in the semantic grid) using MOVE actions that attempt to shift it one 3-pixel cell in the chosen direction; movement could be blocked by walls ('#') or other objects, and pushable blocks ('B') could be moved if they were directly adjacent in the movement direction and had space to shift. Some levels contained multiple selectables ('S'); control could be switched by using CLICK with the exact pixel-coordinate position of the target selectable, after which MOVE actions applied to that newly selected piece. The movement.blocked flags were only hints (indicating adjacency) and did not perfectly predict whether a move would succeed.
